title: Request for disconnection of the power supply - Create

ancestors.title: Request for disconnection of the power supply

storage.value:
Process flow
https://app.diagrams.net/#G1IuUFVOhGtcjb1BynQdmBhu5BAqWt8P83#{"pageId"%3A"QYkEY6YkH-_MGkGUIUQI"}

Charge fee process

https://app.diagrams.net/?splash=0#G1Lnk3MdD3DByEpPN8s1-BCLJC3H4yxrr2#{"pageId"%3A"JsA6_ep1HPjiPbuoDtFs"}

User Story

 

As a system user

I want to be able to create Request for disconnection of the power supply object in the system

So that I will have possibility to timely terminate PODs and inform grid operator and respective customer about the supply disconnection

 

Design: Request for disconnection of the power supply (adobe.com)

Acceptance criteria:

  1. Creating a Request for disconnection of the power supply is possible from Request for disconnection of the power supply's listing page by clicking on a 'Plus' button

  2. Clicking on a Plus button will open Request for disconnection of the power supply's creation page

  3. Check system user permissions to be able to create a new request for disconnection of the power supply

    1. For creating such object, user should have any permissions listed here:

      1. Create Request for disconnection of the power supply as a Draft

        1. Enables to create object only as a draft and do not start disconnection process

      2. Create Request for disconnection of the power supply as Execute

        1. Enables to create object and it is possible to start disconnection process

    2. Enable Plus button when user has one of the permissions

    3. Disable Plus button when user does not have any of the mentioned permissions

  4. Default state of the Request for disconnection of the power supply object contains fields:

    1. Basic Parameter’s tab:

    2. Number

      1. Always disabled

      2. Empty on creation page

      3. Text field

      4. After creating an object, system should display its number with the following logic:

        1. Disconnection-ID of newly created object (For example, Disconnection-56)

          1. In the given example, Disconnection is hardcoded prefix for number and 56 is ID of an object

    3. Date

      1. Always disabled

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. By default, should set current day of the creation

    4. Supplier type

      1. Mandatory

      2. Radio buttons with two hardcoded values:

        1. Current

        2. Previous

    5. Status

      1. Mandatory

      2. Always disabled

      3. Empty on creation page

      4. Three hardcoded values:

        1. Draft

        2. Executed

        3. Fee Charged

      5. System should automatically set a value according to the status of the object.

        1. If it is saved as a draft should set Draft

        2. If it is saved and executed should be set Executed

        3. If taxes are created should be set to Fee charged

    6. Grid operator

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values will be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display Search Bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. Search Grid operator by its name.

    7. Reason for disconnection

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable form nomenclature

        2. Active values will be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display Search Bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. search Reason for disconnection by its name.

    8. Date of registration of the request to the grid operator

      1. Mandatory

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. By default, system should set current day

      6. It is possible to select day from the past or future

      7. System should sent notification to the grid operator about disconnection in this selected date

    9. Date and time of reminder sent to the customer

      1. Mandatory

      2. Disabled

      3. Date picker

      4. Format: DD.MM.YYYY HH:MM

      5. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      6. System should automatically fill according Reminder for power supply disconnection sending date and time

    10. Date to pay the grid operator disconnection fee

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. Date to pay the grid operator disconnection fee > = current date

    11. Date of disconnection of the power supply

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. Date of disconnection of the power supply >= current date

    12. Liability amount from

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-15 characters, 5 digits after decimal point

      5. When the amount is defined, it means to select from Reminders list PODs with the SUM of liabilities more than this defined amount

        1. If amount from and to are not defined, it means to include all the liabilities and do not filter them with its amount

    13. Liability amount to

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-15 characters, 5 digits after decimal point

      5. When the amount is defined, it means to select from Reminders list PODs with the SUM of liabilities less than this defined amount

        1. If amount from and to are not defined, it means to include all the liabilities and do not filter them with its amount

    14. Currency

      1. Disabled when Liability amount from or Liability amount to is not defined

      2. Mandatory and enabled when Liability amount from or Liability amount to is defined

      3. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values should be returned

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display search bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. Search CCY By its name.

      4. If user select different currency value from the liabilities currency value, it means to use exchange rates from the nomenclature the define the current amount of the liability with the chosen Currency value in the Request for disconnection of the power supply object

    15. Customer conditions

      1. Radio buttons

      2. Mandatory

      3. Hardcoded values:

        1. All customers

        2. Customers under conditions

        3. List of customers

      4. Selecting 'All customers' options means to selects all liabilities for all the customer in the system which are overdue and are not fully paid and selects PODs which are included in the invoices for which the liability was created automatically

      5. Selecting 'Customer under conditions' option enabled conditions section and it is possible to define details for the customer and according to the defined conditions system should selects applicable contracts/PODs for disconnection

      6. Selecting 'List of customers' option enables List of Customers text box and it is possible to define Customer IDs and select applicable contracts/PODs for defined customers for disconnection

      7. Customer conditions

        1. Section is enable and mandatory only when the 'Customer under conditions' option is selected

        2. Section is disabled and empty when 'All customer' or 'List of customers' options is selected

        3. Conditions logical operators:

          1. = equality operator. Return true if condition 1 equals to condition 2. Return false if condition 1 does not equal condition 2.

          2. > Greater then operator. Return  true if condition 1 is greater than condition 2. Return false if condition 1 less/equal then condition 2.

          3. <less then operator. Return true if condition 1 is less then condition 2. Return false if condition 1 is more/equal then condition 2.

          4. <> not equality operator. Return true if condition 1 does not equal to condition 2. Return false if condition 1 equals condition 2.

          5. In attribute. Return true if condition 1 equals one of the value from list which is added in attribute. Return false if condition 1 does not equal one of the value from list which is added in attribute

          6. Should have brackets after attribute, to separate more than one value in brackets use comma

          7. And logical operator. Return true if condition 1 and condition 2 both are true. Return false if condition 1 or condition 2 is false.

          8. Or logical operator. Return true if condition 1 or condition 2 are true. Return false if condition 1 and condition 2 are false.

          9. Not attribute. Can be used before any condition and should have brackets after attribute. Return the opposite Boolean of condition

          10. () brackets. Can be used for identification which condition should be checked firstly.

          11. Validate. Validate formula format and displays error if format is wrong.

        4. Variable dropdown

          1. Single select dropdown

          2. Mandatory

          3. Values should be different in the Values dropdown according to the chosen variable

          4. Variable list:

            1. Contract

            2. Product

            3. Service

            4. Product Type

            5. Service Type

            6. Customer Type

            7. Grid operator

            8. POD type

            9. Contract Type

            10. Measurement Type

            11. Voltage level

            12. Purpose of consumption

            13. Interim and advanced payment

            14. Segment

            15. Customer number

            16. Collection channel

          5. Values dropdown

            1. In the values dropdown values should be filtered according to the chosen variable with the following logic:

            2. If Contract is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Product Contract

              2. Service Contract

            3. If Product is selected as a variable, system should display all the product list in the dropdown with its name

              1. System shouldn't display deleted product in the dropdown

              2. It should be possible to search product with its name

              3. Products list should be sorted with its creation date

            4. If Service is selected as a variable, system should display all the services list in the dropdown with its name

              1. System shouldn't display deleted services in the dropdown

              2. It should be possible to search services with its name

              3. Services list should be sorted with its creation date

            5. If Product Type is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Product Type nomenclature

            6. If Service Type is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Service Type nomenclature

            7. If Customer Type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Legal

              2. Private

            8. If Grid Operator is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Grid Operator nomenclature

            9. If POD type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Consumer

              2. Generator

            10. If Contract Type is selected as a variable, system should display four hardcoded values:

              1. Combined

              2. Supply and balancing

              3. Supply only

              4. Without supply

            11. If Measurement Type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. By settlement period

              2. SLP

            12. If Voltage Level is selected as a variable, system should display four hardcoded values:

              1. Low

              2. Medium

              3. Medium - direct connected

              4. High

            13. If Purpose of Consumption is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Household

              2. Non-household

            14. If Interim and Advance Payment is selected as a variable, system should display

              1. Yes

                1. If YES option is selected, it means that the product/service which is connected to the contract has interim and advance payment added

              2. No

                1. If NO option is selected, it means that the product/service which is connected to the contract does not have interim and advance payment added

            15. If Segment is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Segment nomenclature

            16. If Customer Number is selected as a variable, Values dropdown should be disabled and system shouldn't display any values in it

              1. If user selects Customer number as a variable, then in the text box should define additional details in order to start process correctly.

                1. For example, if there is a condition: (Customer number > 0000000001)AND(Customer number<0000000007), system should take all the customer with numbers in this range and start  process for them

            17. If Collection Channel is selected as a variable, system should display all values from collection channels

      8. List of Customers

        1. Disabled and empty when 'All customers' or 'Customer under conditions' option is selected

        2. Enable and mandatory when 'List of customers' option is selected

          1. Text box

          2. Allowed symbols: All symbols are allowed, should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          3. Length: 1-1024 characters

          4. In this text box user should define customer identifiers and selected documents according to the type will be blocked for those customers

          5. If incorrect format of customer ID is defined in the text box, return error

    16. Load PODs for disconnection of the power supply

      1. Button should be disabled if mandatory fields are not defined in the object

      2. Button is enabled when mandatory fields are defined in the object

      3. When user clicks on this button, system should display Listing on the second tab of the object with the PODs which are affected

      4. If List of PODs is generated by clicking “Load PODs for disconnection” button, it should be disabled.

        1. enable if user changes anything on the first tab.

        2. Mandatory for Saving the changes.

          1. “Save as draft” button is disabled before User loads PODs for disconnection again.

          2. “Save and execute” button is disabled before User loads PODs for disconnection again.

          3. only “Cancel” button is enabled on this state.

        3. After user clicks “Load PODS for disconnection” again, “Save as Draft” and “Save and execute” buttons should be enabled.

    17. Sub-objects

      1. Reminder for disconnection

        1. Mandatory

        2. Only one object can be added

        3. Reminder for disconnection of the power supply is added manually by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When user clicks on a Plus button modal window should be opened with one single select dropdown:

            1. In the dropdown Reminder for disconnection of the power supply objects should be displayed with its name and ordered by creation date (newest first)

            2. Do not display Deleted objects.

            3. Display only Executed objects.

            4. Do not display Reminder for disconnection object if all it’s PODS are checked in the Executed Request for disconnection.

            5. It is possible to search object by its name

        4. One Reminder for disconnection can be linked to more than one Requests for disconnection. until all the PODs are selected.

        5. After adding Reminder for disconnection of the power supply it should be displayed with its number

        6. It is possible to go to the detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        7. It is possible to delete already added object by clicking on a delete icon

      2. Request template

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add more than one templates to the object

        3. Clicking on Plus button will open

        4.  a modal window with single select dropdown, from where it should be possible to choose a template

          1. Templates should be displayed with its name

          2. It is possible to search templates with its name

        5. It is possible to download attached template

        6. It is possible to delete selected template from the object by clicking on delete icon

        7. When Request template is attached to the object, it means system to generate a file according to the template after clicking on 'Save' button

        8. Already generated file should be sent to the grid operator to the defined email address from the nomenclature

      3. Tasks

        1. Always disabled on creation mode

        2. Will be enable on Edit and Preview modes

        3. Tasks displaying logic / format:

          1. In the task sub-object, a maximum of 10 tasks should be displayed. If the number of tasks exceeds 10, pagination should be shown. If there are 10 or fewer tasks, pagination should not be visible.

          2. Task list logic:

            1. The tasks ordering should be descending by time creation

            2. Also the task format should be changed, we need to have the next format:

              1. Id

                1. Take from task number field - This logic is already added

              2. Display task status

                1. Task status should be taken from task object

                2. In the system we have the next task statuses:

                  1. Open

                  2. In Progress

                  3. Terminated

                  4. Overdue

                  5. Completed

              3. Date in format DD.MM.YYYY

                1. This logic is already added.

              4. Task type

                1. Task type should be taken from task type dropdown - this logic is already added

              5. Example:

                1. 841 - in progress – 26.02.2025 - YOLS-TEST

      4. Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply

        1. Always disabled

        2. Empty on creation page

        3. System should automatically add Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply object in which this Request is added

        4. It is possible to go to the Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply's detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        5. It is not possible to delete already set record

      5. Payment of the disconnection fees to the grid operator

        1. Always disabled

        2. Empty on creation page

        3. System should automatically add Payment of the disconnection fees to the grid operator object in which this Request is added

        4. It is possible to go to the Payment of the disconnection fees to the grid operator's detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        5. It is not possible to delete already set record

      6. Disconnection of the power supply

        1. Always disabled

        2. Empty on creation page

        3. System should automatically add Disconnection of the power supply object in which this Request is added

        4. It is possible to go to the Disconnection of the power supply's detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        5. It is not possible to delete already set record

      7. Reconnection of the power supply

        1. Always disabled

        2. Empty on creation page

        3. System should automatically add Reconnection of the power supply object in which this Request is added

        4. It is possible to go to the Reconnection of the power supply's detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        5. It is not possible to delete already set record

      8. Files

        1. Always Enabled

        2. Optional

        3. Files can be added manually by the user

        4. In this sub-object system should display generated documents according to the Template when user clicks on 'Save' button

        5. Adding file is possible by clicking on a Plus icon

        6. It is possible by the user to upload more than one file

        7. It is possible to preview files when clicking on its name or an arrow

        8. It is possible to download files after clicking on file sub object

        9. It is possible to delete files – only added manually, generated by the system files cannot be deleted

        10. After adding the file, it should be displayed with file name

        10.  Max size: 50 MB

      9. For Executed objects it is possible to edit only:

        1. Files – to be able to upload new files or to delete manually uploaded files

        2. Button Charge fee is accessible for Executed object

      10. For manual adding sub object -

        1. For each object listed above table, should work following logic:

          1. File sub-object → after clicking file uploading plus button should be displayed pop up window, which contains:

            1. Title “Document details”

              1. Checkbox Draft

                1. Optional

              2. Checkbox Signed

                1. Optional

        2. OK and Cancel buttons,

        3. After clicking on save button , should be opened File uploading pop up window and after uploading file should be save file with selected radio buttons values.

        4. Editing options after uploading files is not possible, the system user should delete the file and add again correctly.

        5. It should possible download file, after uploading.

        6. Document file format as sub-object:

          1. Format:

            1. Contract-25252225.pdf (Draft, Not signed)

            2. Contract-25252.pdf (Draft, Signed

            3. Contract-25225.pdf (Not signed)

            4. Contract-252522.pdf (Signed)

          2. On hover → 09.08.2024 13:23 Username (Name of the user)

    18. Point of delivery tab:

    19. Listing

      1. After user clicks on Load customer for disconnection of the power supply button system should display information is the table according to the selected Reminder for power supply disconnection and filters (Customer condition, Customer liability from, Customer liability to, Grid operator)

        1. If selected PODs are for different grid operators compared to the selected value in the object, such PODs shouldn't be displayed

        2. If PODs SUM of liabilities' current amounts are not in the range defined in the object, such PODs should be excluded

        3. If in the reminders list there are more than one customers with Liabilities from the same POD, only the latest customer should be Displayed in the POD tab of Request for disconnection. display only the customer with the latest Liability for this POD. Example:

          1. Customer-1

            1. Liability-1, Liability-2, Liability-3,

            2. POD-1

          2. Customer-2

            1. Liability-4, Liability-5, Liability-6,

            2. POD-1

          3. We should find the latest created liability from the Liability-1, Liability-2, Liability-3, Liability-4, Liability-5, Liability-6.

          4. If Latest liability is 1,2 or 3, The Customer-1 should be shown in this list.

          5. if latest liability is 4,5 or 6, The customer-2 should be shown in this list.

      2. Searching is possible:

        1. All

        2. Customer identifier

        3. Customer number

        4. Contract number

        5. Billing group number

        6. POD Identifier

        7. Liability number

        8. Outgoing document number

      3. Listing includes columns:

        1. Customer

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display each selected respective customer separately in the listing

          3. Customer should be displayed with its identifier and name + legal form in format 1212112112 (NAME JSC)

        2. Contract

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display only the Product Contracts in this section

          3. System should display product contracts according to the selected configurations

          4. Contracts should be displayed according to the customer

            1. For example, if customer has 3 product contracts and only two of them are respective for the configurations, system should display those 2 product contracts only

          5. Contract should be displayed with its number

        3. Alternative Recipient of an invoice

          1. Optional

          2. System should display Alternative Recipient of an invoice from the Billing Group if defined

            1. should be displayed with its identifier and name + legal form in format 1212112112 (NAME JSC)

            2. If not defined, empty

        4. Billing Group

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display all the billing groups for the displayed product contracts separately

          3. Billing group should be displayed with its number

        5. Point of Delivery

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display all the PODs according to the contract and billing group separately

          3. PODs which are Blocked for Disconnection (such checkbox is selected in the POD object) shouldn’t be displayed

          4. If POD does not belong to the selected Grid operator in the object, such PODs shouldn’t be displayed at all

          5. PODs should be displayed with its number

        6. POD with highest consumption

          1. In the “Request for disconnection” In the second tab, the system displays all pods that should be disconnected. The list shows liabilities and their associated pods, grouped by billing group. after clicking on the “Highest consumption pod” button, the system should automatically select the pod with the highest consumption. The system should check this invoice which is connected with this liability and check third tab of invoice.

            1. In the Invoice third tab (Detaild data) . The system should compare “total volumes” of PODs with each other and PODs with the highest “Total volumes” should be selected in the request for disconnection

          2. It possible to unchecked the pods with highest consumption status.

          3. In this column system should display PODs with the highest consumption according to the Billing Group

            1. If there are more than one PODs with the equal amount of the highest consumption in the billing group, in this case system should display the POD which was created latest (Biggest ID)

            2. For other PODs in the Billing group system should display No in row

          4. Ð¡ase 1:

            1. Invoice 1 → Liability 1 - Billing group contains:

              1. POD 1 -. total volumes - 1500

              2. POD 2 - total volumes - 2000

              3. POD 3 - total volumes - 4020

              4. In the request for disconnection POD 3 should be selected as highest consumption pod

        7. Liabilities in Billing Group

          1. In this column system should display the overdue liabilities which are created for this billing group separately for each POD

          2. Liability should be displayed with the following format:

            1. Amount-Currency-Number

        8. Liabilities in POD

          1. In this column system should display the overdue liabilities which are created for this Billing Group separately for each POD if Separate Invoice checkbox is selected.

            1. If checkbox is not selected, column value is empty

          2. Liability should be displayed with the following format:

            1. Amount-Currency-Number

        9. Existing customer receivables

          1. In this column system should display Yes for customers with existing customer receivables with current amount greater than 0

          2. For other customers should display No

    20. Select all PODs

      1. Button is enabled and optional after the listing is displayed

      2. When user clicks this button, system should select all the listing values which are displayed

        1. After selecting all the PODs it should be possible to unselect any row manually

    21. Select PODs with highest consumption

      1. Button is enabled and optional after the listing is displayed

      2. When user clicks on this button, system should automatically select all the PODs with the highest consumption according the billing group

        1. In this case such row should be automatically selected if not selected

      3. It is possible to manually unselect already selected row after clicking on this button

  5. Save as a draft button

    1. Enable if user has such permission

    2. Disabled if user does not have such permission

    3. Clicking on Save as a draft button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

      1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new Request for disconnection of the power supply object and display it in the listing page (shouldn't start process until its status is Draft)

      2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

      3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

    4. When object is saved as a Draft and user opens it (preview/edit modes), system should check if the Overdue liabilities are paid or not.

      1. If paid, such respective rows from the listing should be removed

    5. When object is saved as a Draft and user opens it (preview/edit modes), system should check if there are another Overdue liabilities found in the system according to the conditions and should display additional rows in the listing

  6. Save and Execute button

    1. Enable if user has such permission

    2. Disabled if user does not have such permission

    3. Clicking on Save and execute button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

      1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new Request for disconnection of the power supply object and display it in the listing page (it is possible to start process of generating documents for Grid operator. Change status to Executed)

      2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

      3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

    4. When object is saved and Executed and user opens it on edit/preview modes, only the already executed data should be displayed in the listing and system shouldn't check anything

  7. When clicking on 'Cancel' or 'X' button warning pop-up message should appear: 'Are you sure you want to cancel?' Yes/No

    1. Yes - will close pop-up and user is returned to the listing page

    2. No - will close pop-up and user stays on the current page

    3. Warning message should be managed from the system settings

Customer feedback:

https://oppa-support.atlassian.net/browse/PDT-1881?search_id=4ab76ef4-d684-45ed-bcb3-cfa0502120d2

view.value:
Process flow
https://app.diagrams.net/#G1IuUFVOhGtcjb1BynQdmBhu5BAqWt8P83#{"pageId"%3A"QYkEY6YkH-_MGkGUIUQI"}

Charge fee process

https://app.diagrams.net/?splash=0#G1Lnk3MdD3DByEpPN8s1-BCLJC3H4yxrr2#{"pageId"%3A"JsA6_ep1HPjiPbuoDtFs"}

User Story

 

As a system user

I want to be able to create Request for disconnection of the power supply object in the system

So that I will have possibility to timely terminate PODs and inform grid operator and respective customer about the supply disconnection

 

Design: Request for disconnection of the power supply (adobe.com)

Acceptance criteria:

  1. Creating a Request for disconnection of the power supply is possible from Request for disconnection of the power supply's listing page by clicking on a 'Plus' button

  2. Clicking on a Plus button will open Request for disconnection of the power supply's creation page

  3. Check system user permissions to be able to create a new request for disconnection of the power supply

    1. For creating such object, user should have any permissions listed here:

      1. Create Request for disconnection of the power supply as a Draft

        1. Enables to create object only as a draft and do not start disconnection process

      2. Create Request for disconnection of the power supply as Execute

        1. Enables to create object and it is possible to start disconnection process

    2. Enable Plus button when user has one of the permissions

    3. Disable Plus button when user does not have any of the mentioned permissions

  4. Default state of the Request for disconnection of the power supply object contains fields:

    1. Basic Parameter’s tab:

    2. Number

      1. Always disabled

      2. Empty on creation page

      3. Text field

      4. After creating an object, system should display its number with the following logic:

        1. Disconnection-ID of newly created object (For example, Disconnection-56)

          1. In the given example, Disconnection is hardcoded prefix for number and 56 is ID of an object

    3. Date

      1. Always disabled

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. By default, should set current day of the creation

    4. Supplier type

      1. Mandatory

      2. Radio buttons with two hardcoded values:

        1. Current

        2. Previous

    5. Status

      1. Mandatory

      2. Always disabled

      3. Empty on creation page

      4. Three hardcoded values:

        1. Draft

        2. Executed

        3. Fee Charged

      5. System should automatically set a value according to the status of the object.

        1. If it is saved as a draft should set Draft

        2. If it is saved and executed should be set Executed

        3. If taxes are created should be set to Fee charged

    6. Grid operator

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values will be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display Search Bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. Search Grid operator by its name.

    7. Reason for disconnection

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable form nomenclature

        2. Active values will be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display Search Bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. search Reason for disconnection by its name.

    8. Date of registration of the request to the grid operator

      1. Mandatory

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. By default, system should set current day

      6. It is possible to select day from the past or future

      7. System should sent notification to the grid operator about disconnection in this selected date

    9. Date and time of reminder sent to the customer

      1. Mandatory

      2. Disabled

      3. Date picker

      4. Format: DD.MM.YYYY HH:MM

      5. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      6. System should automatically fill according Reminder for power supply disconnection sending date and time

    10. Date to pay the grid operator disconnection fee

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. Date to pay the grid operator disconnection fee > = current date

    11. Date of disconnection of the power supply

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. Date of disconnection of the power supply >= current date

    12. Liability amount from

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-15 characters, 5 digits after decimal point

      5. When the amount is defined, it means to select from Reminders list PODs with the SUM of liabilities more than this defined amount

        1. If amount from and to are not defined, it means to include all the liabilities and do not filter them with its amount

    13. Liability amount to

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-15 characters, 5 digits after decimal point

      5. When the amount is defined, it means to select from Reminders list PODs with the SUM of liabilities less than this defined amount

        1. If amount from and to are not defined, it means to include all the liabilities and do not filter them with its amount

    14. Currency

      1. Disabled when Liability amount from or Liability amount to is not defined

      2. Mandatory and enabled when Liability amount from or Liability amount to is defined

      3. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values should be returned

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display search bar if number of items are more than 10

          1. Search CCY By its name.

      4. If user select different currency value from the liabilities currency value, it means to use exchange rates from the nomenclature the define the current amount of the liability with the chosen Currency value in the Request for disconnection of the power supply object

    15. Customer conditions

      1. Radio buttons

      2. Mandatory

      3. Hardcoded values:

        1. All customers

        2. Customers under conditions

        3. List of customers

      4. Selecting 'All customers' options means to selects all liabilities for all the customer in the system which are overdue and are not fully paid and selects PODs which are included in the invoices for which the liability was created automatically

      5. Selecting 'Customer under conditions' option enabled conditions section and it is possible to define details for the customer and according to the defined conditions system should selects applicable contracts/PODs for disconnection

      6. Selecting 'List of customers' option enables List of Customers text box and it is possible to define Customer IDs and select applicable contracts/PODs for defined customers for disconnection

      7. Customer conditions

        1. Section is enable and mandatory only when the 'Customer under conditions' option is selected

        2. Section is disabled and empty when 'All customer' or 'List of customers' options is selected

        3. Conditions logical operators:

          1. = equality operator. Return true if condition 1 equals to condition 2. Return false if condition 1 does not equal condition 2.

          2. > Greater then operator. Return  true if condition 1 is greater than condition 2. Return false if condition 1 less/equal then condition 2.

          3. <less then operator. Return true if condition 1 is less then condition 2. Return false if condition 1 is more/equal then condition 2.

          4. <> not equality operator. Return true if condition 1 does not equal to condition 2. Return false if condition 1 equals condition 2.

          5. In attribute. Return true if condition 1 equals one of the value from list which is added in attribute. Return false if condition 1 does not equal one of the value from list which is added in attribute

          6. Should have brackets after attribute, to separate more than one value in brackets use comma

          7. And logical operator. Return true if condition 1 and condition 2 both are true. Return false if condition 1 or condition 2 is false.

          8. Or logical operator. Return true if condition 1 or condition 2 are true. Return false if condition 1 and condition 2 are false.

          9. Not attribute. Can be used before any condition and should have brackets after attribute. Return the opposite Boolean of condition

          10. () brackets. Can be used for identification which condition should be checked firstly.

          11. Validate. Validate formula format and displays error if format is wrong.

        4. Variable dropdown

          1. Single select dropdown

          2. Mandatory

          3. Values should be different in the Values dropdown according to the chosen variable

          4. Variable list:

            1. Contract

            2. Product

            3. Service

            4. Product Type

            5. Service Type

            6. Customer Type

            7. Grid operator

            8. POD type

            9. Contract Type

            10. Measurement Type

            11. Voltage level

            12. Purpose of consumption

            13. Interim and advanced payment

            14. Segment

            15. Customer number

            16. Collection channel

          5. Values dropdown

            1. In the values dropdown values should be filtered according to the chosen variable with the following logic:

            2. If Contract is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Product Contract

              2. Service Contract

            3. If Product is selected as a variable, system should display all the product list in the dropdown with its name

              1. System shouldn't display deleted product in the dropdown

              2. It should be possible to search product with its name

              3. Products list should be sorted with its creation date

            4. If Service is selected as a variable, system should display all the services list in the dropdown with its name

              1. System shouldn't display deleted services in the dropdown

              2. It should be possible to search services with its name

              3. Services list should be sorted with its creation date

            5. If Product Type is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Product Type nomenclature

            6. If Service Type is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Service Type nomenclature

            7. If Customer Type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Legal

              2. Private

            8. If Grid Operator is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Grid Operator nomenclature

            9. If POD type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Consumer

              2. Generator

            10. If Contract Type is selected as a variable, system should display four hardcoded values:

              1. Combined

              2. Supply and balancing

              3. Supply only

              4. Without supply

            11. If Measurement Type is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. By settlement period

              2. SLP

            12. If Voltage Level is selected as a variable, system should display four hardcoded values:

              1. Low

              2. Medium

              3. Medium - direct connected

              4. High

            13. If Purpose of Consumption is selected as a variable, system should display two hardcoded values:

              1. Household

              2. Non-household

            14. If Interim and Advance Payment is selected as a variable, system should display

              1. Yes

                1. If YES option is selected, it means that the product/service which is connected to the contract has interim and advance payment added

              2. No

                1. If NO option is selected, it means that the product/service which is connected to the contract does not have interim and advance payment added

            15. If Segment is selected as a variable, system should display all the active values from the Segment nomenclature

            16. If Customer Number is selected as a variable, Values dropdown should be disabled and system shouldn't display any values in it

              1. If user selects Customer number as a variable, then in the text box should define additional details in order to start process correctly.

                1. For example, if there is a condition: (Customer number > 0000000001)AND(Customer number<0000000007), system should take all the customer with numbers in this range and start  process for them

            17. If Collection Channel is selected as a variable, system should display all values from collection channels

      8. List of Customers

        1. Disabled and empty when 'All customers' or 'Customer under conditions' option is selected

        2. Enable and mandatory when 'List of customers' option is selected

          1. Text box

          2. Allowed symbols: All symbols are allowed, should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          3. Length: 1-1024 characters

          4. In this text box user should define customer identifiers and selected documents according to the type will be blocked for those customers

          5. If incorrect format of customer ID is defined in the text box, return error

    16. Load PODs for disconnection of the power supply

      1. Button should be disabled if mandatory fields are not defined in the object

      2. Button is enabled when mandatory fields are defined in the object

      3. When user clicks on this button, system should display Listing on the second tab of the object with the PODs which are affected

      4. If List of PODs is generated by clicking “Load PODs for disconnection” button, it should be disabled.

        1. enable if user changes anything on the first tab.

        2. Mandatory for Saving the changes.

          1. “Save as draft” button is disabled before User loads PODs for disconnection again.

          2. “Save and execute” button is disabled before User loads PODs for disconnection again.

          3. only “Cancel” button is enabled on this state.

        3. After user clicks “Load PODS for disconnection” again, “Save as Draft” and “Save and execute” buttons should be enabled.

    17. Sub-objects

      1. Reminder for disconnection

        1. Mandatory

        2. Only one object can be added

        3. Reminder for disconnection of the power supply is added manually by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When user clicks on a Plus button modal window should be opened with one single select dropdown:

            1. In the dropdown Reminder for disconnection of the power supply objects should be displayed with its name and ordered by creation date (newest first)

            2. Do not display Deleted objects.

            3. Display only Executed objects.

            4. Do not display Reminder for disconnection object if all it’s PODS are checked in the Executed Request for disconnection.

            5. It is possible to search object by its name

        4. One Reminder for disconnection can be linked to more than one Requests for disconnection. until all the PODs are selected.

        5. After adding Reminder for disconnection of the power supply it should be displayed with its number

        6. It is possible to go to the detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

        7. It is possible to delete already added object by clicking on a delete icon

      2. Request template

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add more than one templates to the object

        3. Clicking on Plus button will open

        4.  a modal window with single select dropdown, from where it should be possible to choose a template

          1. Templates should be displayed with its name

          2. It is possible to search templates with its name

        5. It is possible to download attached template

        6. It is possible to delete selected template from the object by clicking on delete icon

        7. When Request template is attached to the object, it means system to generate a file according to the template after clicking on 'Save' button

        8. Already generated file should be sent to the grid operator to the defined email address from the nomenclature

      3. Tasks

        1. Always disabled on creation mode

        2. Will be enable on Edit and Preview modes

        3. Tasks displaying logic / format:

          1. In the task sub-object, a maximum of 10 tasks should be displayed. If the number of tasks exceeds 10, pagination should be shown. If there are 10 or fewer tasks, pagination should not be visible.

          2. Task list logic:

            1. The tasks ordering should be descending by time creation

            2. Also the task format should be changed, we need to have the next format:

              1. Id

                1. Take from task number field - This logic is already added

              2. Display task status

                1. Task status should be taken from task object

                2. In the system we have the next task statuses:

                  1. Open

                  2. In Progress

                  3. Terminated

                  4. Overdue

                  5. Completed

              3. Date in format DD.MM.YYYY

                1. This logic is already added.

              4. Task type

                1. Task type should be taken from task type dropdown - this logic is already added

              5. Example:

                1. 841 - in progress – 26.02.2025 - YOLS-TEST

      4. Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply

        1. Always disabled

        2. Empty on creation page

        3. System should automatically add Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply object in which this Request is added

        4. It is possible to go to the Cancellation of a disconnection of the power supply's detail page by clicking on a label or an arrow

      8. Files

        1. Always Enabled

        2. Optional

        3. Files can be added manually by the user

        4. In this sub-object system should display generated documents according to the Template when user clicks on 'Save' button

        5. Adding file is possible by clicking on a Plus icon

        7. It is possible to preview files when clicking on its name or an arrow

        8. It is possible to download files after clicking on file sub object

        9. It is possible to delete files – only added manually, generated by the system files cannot be deleted

        10. After adding the file, it should be displayed with file name

        10.  Max size: 50 MB

      9. For Executed objects it is possible to edit only:

        1. Files – to be able to upload new files or to delete manually uploaded files

      10. For manual adding sub object -

        1. For each object listed above table, should work following logic:

                1. Optional

    18. Point of delivery tab:

    19. Listing

      1. After user clicks on Load customer for disconnection of the power supply button system should display information is the table according to the selected Reminder for power supply disconnection and filters (Customer condition, Customer liability from, Customer liability to, Grid operator)

        1. If selected PODs are for different grid operators compared to the selected value in the object, such PODs shouldn't be displayed

        2. If PODs SUM of liabilities' current amounts are not in the range defined in the object, such PODs should be excluded

        3. If in the reminders list there are more than one customers with Liabilities from the same POD, only the latest customer should be Displayed in the POD tab of Request for disconnection. display only the customer with the latest Liability for this POD. Example:

          1. Customer-1

            1. Liability-1, Liability-2, Liability-3,

            2. POD-1

          2. Customer-2

            1. Liability-4, Liability-5, Liability-6,

            2. POD-1

          3. We should find the latest created liability from the Liability-1, Liability-2, Liability-3, Liability-4, Liability-5, Liability-6.

          4. If Latest liability is 1,2 or 3, The Customer-1 should be shown in this list.

          5. if latest liability is 4,5 or 6, The customer-2 should be shown in this list.

      2. Searching is possible:

        1. All

        2. Customer identifier

        3. Customer number

        4. Contract number

        5. Billing group number

        6. POD Identifier

        7. Liability number

        8. Outgoing document number

      3. Listing includes columns:

        1. Customer

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display each selected respective customer separately in the listing

          3. Customer should be displayed with its identifier and name + legal form in format 1212112112 (NAME JSC)

        2. Contract

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display only the Product Contracts in this section

          3. System should display product contracts according to the selected configurations

          4. Contracts should be displayed according to the customer

            1. For example, if customer has 3 product contracts and only two of them are respective for the configurations, system should display those 2 product contracts only

          5. Contract should be displayed with its number

        3. Alternative Recipient of an invoice

          1. Optional

          2. System should display Alternative Recipient of an invoice from the Billing Group if defined

            1. should be displayed with its identifier and name + legal form in format 1212112112 (NAME JSC)

            2. If not defined, empty

        4. Billing Group

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display all the billing groups for the displayed product contracts separately

          3. Billing group should be displayed with its number

        5. Point of Delivery

          1. Mandatory

          2. System should display all the PODs according to the contract and billing group separately

          3. PODs which are Blocked for Disconnection (such checkbox is selected in the POD object) shouldn’t be displayed

          4. If POD does not belong to the selected Grid operator in the object, such PODs shouldn’t be displayed at all

          5. PODs should be displayed with its number

        6. POD with highest consumption

          1. In the “Request for disconnection” In the second tab, the system displays all pods that should be disconnected. The list shows liabilities and their associated pods, grouped by billing group. after clicking on the “Highest consumption pod” button, the system should automatically select the pod with the highest consumption. The system should check this invoice which is connected with this liability and check third tab of invoice.

            1. In the Invoice third tab (Detaild data) . The system should compare “total volumes” of PODs with each other and PODs with the highest “Total volumes” should be selected in the request for disconnection

          2. It possible to unchecked the pods with highest consumption status.

          3. In this column system should display PODs with the highest consumption according to the Billing Group

            1. If there are more than one PODs with the equal amount of the highest consumption in the billing group, in this case system should display the POD which was created latest (Biggest ID)

            2. For other PODs in the Billing group system should display No in row

          4. Ð¡ase 1:

            1. Invoice 1 → Liability 1 - Billing group contains:

              1. POD 1 -. total volumes - 1500

              2. POD 2 - total volumes - 2000

              3. POD 3 - total volumes - 4020

              4. In the request for disconnection POD 3 should be selected as highest consumption pod

        7. Liabilities in Billing Group

          1. In this column system should display the overdue liabilities which are created for this billing group separately for each POD

          2. Liability should be displayed with the following format:

            1. Amount-Currency-Number

        8. Liabilities in POD

          1. In this column system should display the overdue liabilities which are created for this Billing Group separately for each POD if Separate Invoice checkbox is selected.

            1. If checkbox is not selected, column value is empty

          2. Liability should be displayed with the following format:

            1. Amount-Currency-Number

        9. Existing customer receivables

          1. In this column system should display Yes for customers with existing customer receivables with current amount greater than 0

          2. For other customers should display No

    20. Select all PODs

      1. Button is enabled and optional after the listing is displayed

      2. When user clicks this button, system should select all the listing values which are displayed

        1. After selecting all the PODs it should be possible to unselect any row manually

    21. Select PODs with highest consumption

      1. Button is enabled and optional after the listing is displayed

      2. When user clicks on this button, system should automatically select all the PODs with the highest consumption according the billing group

        1. In this case such row should be automatically selected if not selected

      3. It is possible to manually unselect already selected row after clicking on this button

  5. Save as a draft button

    1. Enable if user has such permission

    2. Disabled if user does not have such permission

    3. Clicking on Save as a draft button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

      1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new Request for disconnection of the power supply object and display it in the listing page (shouldn't start process until its status is Draft)

      2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

      3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

    4. When object is saved as a Draft and user opens it (preview/edit modes), system should check if the Overdue liabilities are paid or not.

      1. If paid, such respective rows from the listing should be removed

    5. When object is saved as a Draft and user opens it (preview/edit modes), system should check if there are another Overdue liabilities found in the system according to the conditions and should display additional rows in the listing

  6. Save and Execute button

    1. Enable if user has such permission

    2. Disabled if user does not have such permission

    3. Clicking on Save and execute button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

      1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new Request for disconnection of the power supply object and display it in the listing page (it is possible to start process of generating documents for Grid operator. Change status to Executed)

      2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

      3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

    4. When object is saved and Executed and user opens it on edit/preview modes, only the already executed data should be displayed in the listing and system shouldn't check anything

  7. When clicking on 'Cancel' or 'X' button warning pop-up message should appear: 'Are you sure you want to cancel?' Yes/No

    1. Yes - will close pop-up and user is returned to the listing page

    2. No - will close pop-up and user stays on the current page

    3. Warning message should be managed from the system settings
